entity.summary: Historical Aptos on-chain central-limit-order-book protocol designed for hyper-parallelized market creation, order placement, matching, and settlement.
entity.notes: The first-party repository preserves the Econia order-book protocol and documentation but explicitly describes its scripts as not actively maintained. Current registry infrastructure places Econia in the dead-adapter corpus. HEI therefore uses inactive rather than dead because no first-party terminal date or shutdown statement was recovered.

evidence.title: Econia protocol repository
evidence.notes: First-party repository identifies Econia as a hyper-parallelized on-chain order book for Aptos, preserves the Move protocol source and documentation, and notes that project scripts are not actively maintained.

evidence.title: Econia dead-adapter registry classification
evidence.notes: Current protocol-registry infrastructure preserves Econia within its dead-adapter corpus. HEI uses this only to support inactive classification and does not infer an exact terminal date or cause.
